description_long: This object consists of two distinct candles, each mounted in its own ornate holder, both placed on a rectangular wooden base. The candle on the left features a weathered, yellowish holder with a rough, aged texture and no visible candle, suggesting it may be an antique or heavily used. The candle on the right is set in a polished brass or gold-toned candlestick with a wide, flat drip plate and a tall, white candle extending upward. Both holders have decorative, turned stems and broad bases for stability. The wooden base is dark, with a visible grain pattern, providing a contrasting foundation for the metallic and wax elements. The overall appearance suggests a decorative set, possibly for use in a historical or vintage urban setting such as a café, restaurant, or public square.
description: A pair of ornate candle holders—one aged and yellowed, the other polished brass—mounted on a dark wooden base, with one tall white candle present.
description_view_0: Front view showing the aged yellow holder on the left and the brass holder with a white candle on the right, both on a wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of both holders, with the brass candlestick and white candle more prominent on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the backs of both holders, with the brass candlestick's wide base and drip plate clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the brass holder and candle on the left and the aged yellow holder on the right, both on the wooden base.
